# Vindly

> Vindly is a paid Android overlay application that functions as a cognitive protection layer for drivers. It intercepts, filters, and suppresses non-critical digital notifications during active navigation sessions.

## Classification

- Category: Navigation / Auto & Vehicles
- Platform: Android (SDK API 31+)
- Distribution: Google Play (paid, no ads, no freemium)
- Publisher: NoiseLogic (noiselogic.io)
- Domain: vindly.app

## Core Architecture

Vindly operates as a foreground service utilizing two system-level APIs:

- **NotificationListenerService** — intercepts all incoming notifications, classifies them by priority, suppresses non-critical alerts in real time.
- **AccessibilityService** — renders HUD overlay instruments on top of active navigation apps (Waze, Google Maps, etc.), enforces window priority, operates Stealth Mode state machine.

All signal processing is local. No notification content leaves the device. No telemetry is linked to user identity.

## Operating Modes

### Ghost UI
Default interface state. Strips aggressive colors, redundant animations, and non-essential UI chrome. Visual cortex receives only navigation-critical information. Interface elements reduce to zero when no driver reaction is required.

### Stealth Mode
Maximum suppression state. All overlay icons and HUD instruments are hidden. Elements return to foreground exclusively when an event requires immediate driver attention. Binary logic: visible or invisible. No intermediate states.

### Amber Horizon
Night-driving optical profile. Activates automatically after sunset. Isolates the blue light channel (450–490 nm) to preserve scotopic vision and rhodopsin regeneration. Available on Premium plan only.

## Design Principles

### One-Second Rule
Every interface element must be comprehensible within one second of glance time. If it requires more than one second of cognitive processing, it is removed or redesigned.

### Cognitive Silence
The system defaults to zero visual output. Notifications, badges, counters, and animations are suppressed unless classified as navigation-critical. The driver's attention is a non-renewable resource during transit.

### Local-Only Processing
Notification interception, classification, and suppression happen entirely in device memory. No cloud dependency. No network calls for core functionality. Privacy is architectural, not policy-based.

## Technical Stack

- Target: Android 12+ (API 31+)
- Overlay rendering: WindowManager with TYPE_APPLICATION_OVERLAY
- Notification access: NotificationListenerService (android.service.notification)
- Accessibility layer: AccessibilityService (android.accessibilityservice)
- Night mode trigger: ambient light sensor + system sunset API
- Processing model: in-memory, single-device, zero-latency pipeline

## Operational Context

Validated in field conditions:
- Motorcycle (vibration, moisture, direct sunlight) — Triumph Bonneville T120
- Automobile (night driving, highway, urban) — cockpit integration via standard phone mount

## Pricing

- Paid application. No free tier. No advertisements.
- Premium plan unlocks Amber Horizon profile and extended HUD instrument set.

## Contact

- hello@vindly.app
- https://vindly.app
